intro: Slime in the KJV Bible refers to natural bitumen or asphalt — hemar in Hebrew — a petroleum product that seeped naturally to the surface in the Dead Sea region and was used as a waterproof mortar and caulking agent. The builders of Babel used slime (bitumen) for mortar in their brick construction: they had brick for stone and slime for mortar (Genesis 11:3). The Vale of Siddim (southern Dead Sea) was full of slime pits into which the kings of Sodom and Gomorrah fell in their rout (Genesis 14:10). Moses's mother used slime (hemar) along with pitch to waterproof the basket in which she hid the infant Moses among the reeds of the Nile (Exodus 2:3). The bitumen of the Dead Sea region was known in antiquity and exported as a commercial commodity.
